what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note (a comment on another item) in which Tim records a design point about scoping dragnet card outputs by frame, reusing an existing scope ladder, while stating that he does not know what the frames are.
observations: Note type with state 'posted' authored by tim in channel 'dragnet-development'
Created and updated timestamps are identical and include timezone offset
History contains a single 'filed' transition by tim
Body uses a '[point]' tag and quotes a prior suggestion about scoping outputs by frame
Lists four intended visibility levels: session-private, channel-shared, project-shared, global
Ends with an explicit caveat that the author does not know what the frames are
standouts: The author openly states 'I don’t know what the frames are'
The four-tier scope mapping (session / channel / project / global) tied to attachment frames
It is a comment on item-7c7dbc35 rather than a top-level thread
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, timestamps, and history
Uses board:// URI identifiers for items and links
Markdown body with a tagged note style '[point]'
ISO 8601 timestamp format with explicit timezone
notes: Tim's noticeboard point proposes scoping dragnet card outputs by which frame they attach to, reusing an existing scope ladder, but flags that the frame definitions are unknown to him.
questions: What is the existing 'scope ladder' and where is it defined?
What is a 'dragnet card' in this system?
What does the parent item item-7c7dbc35 contain?
How does attachment to a frame map concretely to session/channel/project/global visibility?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to make a design point about scoping outputs by frame; the file does that, but it also reveals the author lacks the frame definitions, so the proposal is under-specified in this note.
apparent_themes: Reuse of existing mechanisms over new ones
Honest signaling of knowledge gaps in design discussion
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to advance a design idea—scope dragnet outputs by attachment frame using an existing ladder—while simultaneously flagging a missing piece of context so the discussion can be clarified.
novelty: It maps four visibility tiers (session-private, channel-shared, project-shared, global) to attachment frames rather than proposing a new scoping mechanism.
contribution: Records a design point that dragnet output scope could be expressed by which frame a card attaches to, leveraging an existing scope ladder, and records the open question of what those frames are.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by tim commenting on item-7c7dbc35, proposing that dragnet card outputs be scoped by attachment frame using an existing scope ladder with session/channel/project/global levels, while noting uncertainty about the frame definitions.
intention_for_embedding: To record a design point and request clarification on the frame model for dragnet output scoping.
